concerns.text: FR-003 mandates a fixed correlation threshold (|r| ≥ 0.3, p ≤ 0.05) for the counterfactual search. The Idea explicitly states in 'Assumptions' that these values 'will be subject to a sensitivity analysis sweeping the range {0.1, 0.3, 0.5}'. The spec hardcodes a single value, contradicting the idea's requirement for a sensitivity analysis to ensure robustness.

US-2 Acceptance Scenario 3 requires the counterfactual query to return a result with 'p-value < 0.05'. This contradicts the Idea's 'Assumptions' which state the threshold will be swept (0.1, 0.3, 0.5). Hardcoding 0.05 in the acceptance criteria prevents the system from testing the robustness of the counterfactual detection across the specified range, effectively narrowing the research scope.

FR-005 mandates a '6-hour execution window on a CPU-only environment with ≤ 7 GB RAM'. The Idea's 'Methodology sketch' mentions 'batched API calls' as a valid alternative to local inference to meet the time budget. The spec restricts the implementation to a specific hardware constraint (CPU-only) that excludes the API-based approach explicitly allowed in the idea, potentially over-constraining the solution space.
